title: Telephony settings discovery
description:
Source flow: TEL-QA-001

Telephony settings are visible under Voice & Video and start disabled unless already configured.
postconditions:
- Screenshot of Voice & Video showing Telephony.
- Note whether this is a fresh or reused profile.
steps_actions:
1. "Launch Rocket.Chat."
2. "In the left vertical server list, click the three-dots/kebab button near the bottom edge below the server buttons, then click `Settings` in the menu that opens."
3. "Click the `Voice & Video` tab."
4. "Find Telephony."
5. "Check initial state."
steps_data:
1. "Agent: Start app and wait for main window."
2. "Alternate path: app menu item `Settings` when the desktop menu bar is visible.
Agent: Navigate to Settings view."
3. "Agent: Select Voice & Video settings tab."
4. "Agent: Search visible text for Telephony."
5. "Agent: Read the telephony toggle state."
steps_results:
1. "Main window is visible."
2. "Settings screen opens."
3. "Voice & Video options are visible."
4. "Telephony section is present."
5. "Toggle is off unless this profile was previously configured."

title: Enable and disable telephony protocol handling
description:
Source flow: TEL-QA-002

Disabled telephony ignores phone links; enabled telephony handles them.
postconditions:
- Screenshot or screen recording showing disabled vs enabled behavior.
- Note any OS prompt shown by the browser.
steps_actions:
1. "In the left vertical server list, click the three-dots/kebab button near the bottom edge below the server buttons, click `Settings`, click the `Voice & Video` tab near the top of Settings, then find the `Telephony` section heading."
2. "Switch the Telephony toggle off."
3. "Open `test-links.html` and click `tel:+15551234567`."
4. "Switch the Telephony toggle on."
5. "Click `tel:+15551234567` again."
6. "Click `callto:+15551234567`."
steps_data:
1. "Agent: Navigate to Telephony settings."
2. "Agent: Set telephony toggle to off."
3. "Agent: Trigger the same link."
4. "Agent: Set telephony toggle to on."
5. "Agent: Trigger the same link."
6. "Agent: Trigger the same link."
steps_results:
1. "Toggle is visible."
2. "Diagnostics section is hidden or inactive."
3. "Rocket.Chat does not place a telephony call request."
4. "Default-handler prompt or diagnostics can appear."
5. "Rocket.Chat opens the telephony dialpad flow."
6. "Rocket.Chat opens the telephony dialpad flow."

title: Default handler prompt
description:
Source flow: TEL-QA-003

Enabling telephony shows a clear default-handler prompt with working actions where supported.
postconditions:
- Screenshot of the prompt.
- On Windows/Linux, screenshot the opened settings page. On macOS, note that no
  settings page is expected.
steps_actions:
1. "In the left vertical server list, click the three-dots/kebab button near the bottom edge below the server buttons, click `Settings`, click the `Voice & Video` tab near the top of Settings, find the `Telephony` section heading, then switch Telephony off."
2. "Switch the Telephony toggle on."
3. "Read prompt copy."
4. "In the visible default-handler prompt modal, click the prompt button labeled `Open Settings` if present."
5. "Close the prompt."
6. "Switch Telephony off, then switch it on again."
steps_data:
1. "Agent: Ensure toggle is off."
2. "Agent: Set toggle to on."
3. "Agent: Capture prompt title/body/buttons."
4. "Agent: Activate Open Settings action."
5. "Agent: Dismiss modal."
6. "Agent: Repeat state transition."
steps_results:
1. "Prompt is not visible."
2. "Prompt opens once for the enable transition."
3. "Copy mentions handling phone links/default app behavior."
4. "Windows/Linux opens default-app settings; macOS action is absent or no-op by design."
5. "Modal closes and app remains usable."
6. "Prompt can appear again on a new off-to-on transition."

title: Telephony diagnostics panel
description:
Source flow: TEL-QA-004

Diagnostics can be expanded, refreshed, copied, and interpreted.
postconditions:
- Paste copied diagnostics JSON into the result note.
- Screenshot any failed row.
steps_actions:
1. "In the left vertical server list, click the three-dots/kebab button near the bottom edge below the server buttons, click `Settings`, click the `Voice & Video` tab near the top of Settings, then find the `Telephony` section heading."
2. "Expand Diagnostics."
3. "Click Refresh."
4. "Click Copy."
5. "Review `isDefault.tel` and `isDefault.callto`."
6. "If a diagnostics row has a button labeled `Open Settings`, click it."
steps_data:
1. "Agent: Navigate to Telephony settings."
2. "Agent: Expand diagnostics panel."
3. "Agent: Activate Refresh."
4. "Agent: Activate Copy."
5. "Agent: Parse copied JSON checks."
6. "Agent: Activate row action."
steps_results:
1. "Diagnostics accordion is visible."
2. "Checks list appears."
3. "Generated timestamp or statuses update."
4. "Clipboard contains diagnostics JSON."
5. "Statuses reflect current OS default-handler state."
6. "OS settings opens where supported."

title: Single workspace tel and callto links
description:
Source flow: TEL-QA-005

Clicking valid phone links opens the dialpad in the only configured workspace.
postconditions:
- Record each link clicked and observed number.
- Screenshot the dialpad state for at least one `tel:` link and one `callto:`
  link.
steps_actions:
1. "Confirm only one workspace is configured."
2. "In the left vertical server list, click the three-dots/kebab button near the bottom edge below the server buttons, click `Settings`, click the `Voice & Video` tab near the top of Settings, find the `Telephony` section heading, then switch Telephony on."
3. "Open `test-links.html` in a browser."
4. "Click each valid `tel:` link."
5. "Click each valid `callto:` link."
6. "Return to Rocket.Chat after each click."
steps_data:
1. "Agent: Count configured servers."
2. "Agent: Set telephony toggle on."
3. "Agent: Open local HTML file."
4. "Agent: Trigger each valid `tel:` URI."
5. "Agent: Trigger each valid `callto:` URI."
6. "Agent: Observe app focus and workspace."
steps_results:
1. "Exactly one workspace exists."
2. "Telephony is enabled."
3. "Link page is visible."
4. "Dialpad receives the normalized number."
5. "Dialpad receives the normalized number."
6. "The only workspace is used; no server picker appears."

title: Multi-workspace server picker
description:
Source flow: TEL-QA-006

The server picker opens, routes to the chosen workspace, and supports cancel.
postconditions:
- Screenshot of server picker.
- Note selected workspace and final dialpad workspace.
steps_actions:
1. "Configure at least two workspaces."
2. "Click `tel:+15551234567`."
3. "Review modal contents."
4. "Click a workspace without Remember checked."
5. "Click another link."
6. "Close/cancel the modal."
steps_data:
1. "Agent: Ensure server list length is at least two."
2. "Agent: Trigger the link."
3. "Agent: Read server rows."
4. "Agent: Select a server with remember false."
5. "Agent: Trigger another phone link."
6. "Agent: Dismiss modal."
steps_results:
1. "Multiple workspace choices are available."
2. "Server picker modal opens."
3. "Each workspace has readable title/host and selectable row."
4. "Dialpad opens in selected workspace."
5. "Picker opens again."
6. "No call request is placed."

title: Preferred server persistence
description:
Source flow: TEL-QA-007

Remembering a workspace skips the picker on later calls and survives restart.
postconditions:
- Note chosen workspace URL/title.
- Record whether restart preserved the choice.
steps_actions:
1. "Start with a fresh profile or clear persisted app settings before launching Rocket.Chat."
2. "Click `tel:+15551234567`."
3. "Check Remember choice and select workspace A."
4. "Click the same link again."
5. "Quit and relaunch Rocket.Chat."
6. "Click the same link again."
7. "Remove or make workspace A unavailable."
steps_data:
1. "Agent: Start from no preferred server."
2. "Agent: Trigger link."
3. "Agent: Select server with remember true."
4. "Agent: Trigger link again."
5. "Agent: Restart app."
6. "Agent: Trigger link again."
7. "Agent: Simulate stale preferred server if practical."
steps_results:
1. "First link opens picker."
2. "Server picker opens."
3. "Dialpad opens in workspace A."
4. "Picker is skipped; workspace A is used."
5. "App starts normally."
6. "Picker is still skipped; workspace A is used."
7. "Picker opens instead of silently failing."

title: Telephony global shortcut
description:
Source flow: TEL-QA-008

Configured shortcut reads clipboard on trigger and opens the telephony flow.
postconditions:
- Screenshot shortcut configuration and any error state.
- Record accelerator used.
steps_actions:
1. "In the left vertical server list, click the three-dots/kebab button near the bottom edge below the server buttons, click `Settings`, click the `Voice & Video` tab near the top of Settings, then find the `Telephony` section heading."
2. "Enable shortcut and set a non-conflicting accelerator."
3. "Copy `+15551234567` to clipboard."
4. "Press the shortcut."
5. "Copy `tel:+55 11 99999-1234`."
6. "Press the shortcut."
7. "Copy invalid text."
8. "Press the shortcut."
9. "Try a reserved/conflicting shortcut."
steps_data:
1. "Agent: Navigate to Telephony settings."
2. "Agent: Configure shortcut."
3. "Agent: Set clipboard text."
4. "Agent: Dispatch accelerator."
5. "Agent: Set clipboard text."
6. "Agent: Dispatch accelerator."
7. "Agent: Set clipboard text to `not a phone`."
8. "Agent: Dispatch accelerator."
9. "Agent: Configure known conflict if safe."
steps_results:
1. "Global shortcut controls are visible."
2. "Registration status shows success or no error."
3. "Clipboard contains phone number."
4. "Dialpad opens with `+15551234567`."
5. "Clipboard contains URI."
6. "Dialpad opens with `+5511999991234`."
7. "Clipboard contains invalid text."
8. "Dialpad opens with empty input; no malformed number is sent."
9. "UI reports failure without crashing."

title: macOS cold launch from tel and callto links
description:
Source flow: TEL-QA-009

Clicking a phone link while Rocket.Chat is closed launches the app and routes the link.
postconditions:
- Screen recording is preferred because this tests app launch timing.
- Note browser used.
steps_actions:
1. "In the left vertical server list, click the three-dots/kebab button near the bottom edge below the server buttons, click `Settings`, click the `Voice & Video` tab near the top of Settings, find the `Telephony` section heading, then switch Telephony on."
2. "Quit Rocket.Chat completely."
3. "Open `test-links.html` in Safari or Chrome."
4. "Click `tel:+15551234567`."
5. "Quit Rocket.Chat again."
6. "Click `callto:+15551234567`."
7. "Repeat while Rocket.Chat is already running."
steps_data:
1. "Agent: Set telephony toggle on."
2. "Agent: Ensure no Rocket.Chat process remains."
3. "Agent: Open local HTML in browser."
4. "Agent: Trigger link."
5. "Agent: Ensure no process remains."
6. "Agent: Trigger link."
7. "Agent: Trigger link with running app."
steps_results:
1. "App is registered for phone protocols."
2. "App is closed."
3. "Link page is visible."
4. "Rocket.Chat launches and opens telephony flow."
5. "App is closed."
6. "Rocket.Chat launches and opens telephony flow."
7. "Existing app window focuses and routes link."

title: Windows Default Apps for tel and callto
description:
Source flow: TEL-QA-010

Rocket.Chat appears in Windows Default Apps and can own both tel and callto.
postconditions:
- Screenshot Default Apps assignment.
- Copied diagnostics JSON.
steps_actions:
1. "Install the branch Windows build."
2. "In the left vertical server list, click the three-dots/kebab button near the bottom edge below the server buttons, click `Settings`, click the `Voice & Video` tab near the top of Settings, find the `Telephony` section heading, then switch Telephony on."
3. "Open Windows Settings -> Apps -> Default apps."
4. "Search or open Rocket.Chat."
5. "Assign `tel` to Rocket.Chat."
6. "Assign `callto` to Rocket.Chat."
7. "Open Rocket.Chat diagnostics."
8. "Click valid links in `test-links.html`."
steps_data:
1. "Agent: Install app."
2. "Agent: Set telephony toggle on."
3. "Agent: Open Default Apps settings."
4. "Agent: Locate registered app entry."
5. "Agent: Set `tel` protocol default."
6. "Agent: Set `callto` protocol default."
7. "Agent: Read checks."
8. "Agent: Trigger `tel` and `callto`."
steps_results:
1. "Rocket.Chat appears in Start menu/apps."
2. "Prompt or diagnostics is available."
3. "Default Apps window opens."
4. "Rocket.Chat appears as a candidate."
5. "Windows accepts Rocket.Chat."
6. "Windows accepts Rocket.Chat."
7. "`isDefault.tel` and `isDefault.callto` pass."
8. "Rocket.Chat opens telephony flow for both."

title: Windows MSI SET_DEFAULT_ASSOCIATIONS policy
description:
Source flow: TEL-QA-011

MSI policy points to an existing XML and diagnostics pass after Windows applies defaults.
postconditions:
- MSI install log.
- `reg query` output.
- Screenshot or command output proving XML exists.
- Diagnostics JSON.
steps_actions:
1. "Install MSI with `SET_DEFAULT_ASSOCIATIONS=1`."
2. "Query policy registry value."
3. "Check the XML path exists."
4. "Sign out and sign back in after install."
5. "In the left vertical server list, click the three-dots/kebab button near the bottom edge below the server buttons, click `Settings`, click the `Voice & Video` tab near the top of Settings, find the `Telephony` section heading, then switch Telephony on."
6. "Open diagnostics."
7. "Click valid links."
8. "Uninstall."
steps_data:
1. "Agent: Run `msiexec /i <msi> SET_DEFAULT_ASSOCIATIONS=1 /qn`."
2. "Agent: Run `reg query ""HKLM\\SOFTWARE\\Policies\\Microsoft\\Windows\\System"" /v DefaultAssociationsConfiguration`."
3. "Agent: Test file existence."
4. "Agent: Reapply Windows default associations through a logon cycle."
5. "Agent: Set telephony toggle on."
6. "Agent: Read checks."
7. "Agent: Trigger `tel` and `callto`."
8. "Agent: Remove MSI."
steps_results:
1. "Install succeeds."
2. "Value points to Rocket.Chat XML under install `resources`."
3. "`RocketChatDefaultAppAssociations.xml` exists at the registry path."
4. "Windows applies defaults."
5. "Diagnostics are available."
6. "`isDefault.tel` and `isDefault.callto` pass when policy applied."
7. "Rocket.Chat handles both protocols."
8. "Installer removes policy only if it owns the sentinel."

title: Linux protocol handling
description:
Source flow: TEL-QA-012

Linux desktop protocol defaults can route tel and callto links to Rocket.Chat.
postconditions:
- Diagnostics JSON.
- Desktop environment name.
- Command output from `xdg-mime` if used.
steps_actions:
1. "Install Linux package or run packaged build."
2. "In the left vertical server list, click the three-dots/kebab button near the bottom edge below the server buttons, click `Settings`, click the `Voice & Video` tab near the top of Settings, find the `Telephony` section heading, then switch Telephony on."
3. "Open diagnostics."
4. "If a visible diagnostics row shows a button labeled `Open Settings`, click it."
5. "Set `tel` and `callto` to Rocket.Chat when diagnostics report another handler."
6. "Click valid links in `test-links.html`."
steps_data:
1. "Agent: Install app package."
2. "Agent: Set telephony toggle on."
3. "Agent: Read checks."
4. "Agent: Activate Open Settings action."
5. "Agent: Use desktop settings first; use xdg tools only when desktop settings are unavailable."
6. "Agent: Trigger `tel` and `callto`."
steps_results:
1. "Desktop file is available."
2. "App attempts protocol registration."
3. "`linux.xdg.tel` and `linux.xdg.callto` reflect current defaults."
4. "GNOME/KDE default-app settings opens when supported."
5. "Rocket.Chat owns both handlers."
6. "Rocket.Chat opens telephony flow."

title: Localization and layout smoke
description:
Source flow: TEL-QA-013

Telephony UI is readable without clipping in key locales.
postconditions: - Screenshots for each locale and UI surface.
steps_actions:
1. "Set app/system locale to English."
2. "Check prompt, diagnostics, server picker, shortcut controls."
3. "Repeat in Brazilian Portuguese."
4. "Repeat in German."
5. "Resize window to a narrow supported width."
steps_data:
1. "Agent: Launch with English locale or record that locale switching is unavailable."
2. "Agent: Capture each UI surface."
3. "Agent: Launch with pt-BR or record that locale switching is unavailable."
4. "Agent: Launch with de-DE or record that locale switching is unavailable."
5. "Agent: Set smaller viewport/window."
steps_results:
1. "Telephony settings copy is readable."
2. "No clipping or overlap."
3. "Copy remains readable."
4. "Long labels remain readable."
5. "Text wraps or truncates cleanly."

title: Negative and edge cases
description:
Source flow: TEL-QA-014

Invalid or unsupported inputs fail safely without crashes or wrong calls.
postconditions:
- Notes for each edge input and observed result.
- Screenshots for any unexpected modal or error.
steps_actions:
1. "Disable Telephony and click a valid phone link."
2. "In a fresh profile with zero workspaces, use the left vertical server list and click the three-dots/kebab button near the bottom edge below the server buttons, click `Settings`, click the `Voice & Video` tab near the top of Settings, find the `Telephony` section heading, then switch Telephony on."
3. "Click `tel:` from `test-links.html`."
4. "Click `callto:?subject=empty`."
5. "With multiple workspaces, open picker and cancel."
6. "Trigger two phone links quickly."
7. "Create a stale remembered workspace by remembering a server, then removing that server from the profile."
steps_data:
1. "Agent: Trigger `tel:+15551234567`."
2. "Agent: Use a fresh profile with zero workspaces."
3. "Agent: Trigger empty `tel`."
4. "Agent: Trigger query-only `callto`."
5. "Agent: Trigger link then dismiss modal."
6. "Agent: Fire links in quick succession."
7. "Agent: Remove remembered server from server list."
steps_results:
1. "No call request is placed."
2. "Phone link does not crash; no call is placed."
3. "No call request is placed."
4. "No call request is placed."
5. "No call request is placed."
6. "App avoids duplicate/concurrent modal failures."
7. "Picker opens instead of silently failing."
